input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | Tracks current selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires on selection; navigate here |
| `RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull-to-refresh; reload API data |
| `IsRefreshing` (bool) | Controls refresh spinner |

**ViewModel sketch:**

```csharp
[RelayCommand]
async Task OnSelectionChanged()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
        new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });
    SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task Refresh()
{
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(await _api.GetItemsAsync());
    IsRefreshing = false;
}
```

**Key points:**

- `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView` to enable pull-to-refresh.
- Set `IsRefreshing = false` after loading to dismiss the spinner.
- Null out `SelectedItem` after navigation so the same item can be tapped again.
- `SelectionMode="Single"` prevents multi-select.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh, along with navigation to a detail page on selection. They want XAML and ViewModel binding explanations. This is a straightforward knowledge question - I'll provide the answer directly without needing to search any codebase.
